summary: Add a stable top-level 'data' alias to the enveloped JSON command families (provenance-wrapped reads, list/create/mutate, generate) so consumers can read .data uniformly; non-breaking (existing results/clips/meta keys retained). Does NOT cover the bare printJSONFiltered novel commands (credits, top, tree, lineage, analytics, status, models, grep, sessions, vibes, burn), which emit a bare typed value with no envelope.
reason: Dogfooding showed scripted/agent consumers must guess the payload key per command: list/create/mutate emit {action,data}, endpoint-mirror read commands emit {meta,results} (via wrapWithProvenance), and generate emits {status,clips}. Chose non-breaking convergence (option B): wrapWithProvenance now also emits data mirroring results (covers all provenance-wrapped read commands in one seam), and generate's JSON output adds data mirroring clips. The standard envelope already carried data. Scope note: hand-written novel commands that emit a bare typed slice/struct straight through printJSONFiltered have no envelope at all, so they carry no data alias; giving them one would require wrapping bare JSON arrays, which is the breaking full-standardization (option A/C) deliberately deferred.
